content.thinking:
The logs make it clear: all nodes are running, waiting to join/form a cluster, and waiting for `cockroach init`. The nodes can communicate with each other (at least partially - one is trying to reach peers using DNS names that resolve to Pod IPs).

Looking at the logs:
- All nodes started successfully
- They're all waiting for `cockroach init` or to join an already initialized node
- The join RPCs are failing because connection is refused (but this is expected when none are initialized yet)

The fix is simple: run `cockroach init` on one of the nodes. This is the bootstrap command that initializes the cluster. Once that's done, the other nodes should automatically join.
I should verify the StatefulSet configuration to make sure the `--join` flags are properly set up for the nodes to discover and connect to each other.

content.text:
The diagnosis is clear: all 3 nodes started successfully and are waiting for `cockroach init`. The join RPCs failing with "connection refused" is expected before initialization — the nodes can see each other's DNS names. I just need to run `cockroach init` to bootstrap the cluster.

Let me first verify the StatefulSet's `--join` flags are correct:

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o yaml 2>&1 | grep -A 30 'args:'
input.description: Check StatefulSet args for join configuration

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o yaml 2>&1

spec:
  persistentVolumeClaimRetentionPolicy:
    whenDeleted: Retain
    whenScaled: Retain
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  replicas: 3
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
      app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - exec /cockroach/cockroach start --logtostderr=INFO --insecure --advertise-host=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
          --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 --port=26257 --cache=25% --max-sql-memory=25% --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        - name: POD_NAMESPACE
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              apiVersion: v1
              fieldPath: metadata.namespace
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        livenessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 3
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: http
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        name: db
        ports:
        - containerPort: 26257
          name: grpc
          protocol: TCP
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        readinessProbe:
          failureThreshold: 2
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: http
            scheme: HTTP
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 1
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 2Gi
          requests:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: 2Gi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
          name: datadir
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: crdb-cluster-sa
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 10
  updateStrategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      partition: 0
    type: RollingUpdate
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - apiVersion: v1
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: crdb-cluster
        app.kubernetes.io/name: cockroachdb
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes:
      - ReadWriteOnce
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
      volumeMode: Filesystem
    status:
      phase: Pending
status:
  availableReplicas: 0
  collisionCount: 0
  currentReplicas: 3
  currentRevision: crdb-cluster-76646fb9b9
  observedGeneration: 1
  replicas: 3
  updateRevision: crdb-cluster-76646fb9b9
  updatedReplicas: 3

content.thinking:
The StatefulSet configuration looks correct:
- Each pod uses `--advertise-host=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local`
- The `--join` flag includes all 3 nodes: `crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257`
- The service is named `crdb-cluster` in namespace `cockroachdb`
- Running in insecure mode

The real problem is that the cluster hasn't been initialized yet—the nodes are just waiting for someone to bootstrap it. I need to run the initialization command against one of the pods to get things started.

content.text: The configuration is correct — proper `--join` list with all 3 DNS names, correct `--advertise-host`, insecure mode. The only thing missing is the one-time `cockroach init` bootstrap command. Running it now:
Cluster bootstrapped. Now waiting for all 3 nodes to come up and verify they're healthy:

content.thinking:
The cluster is initializing nicely. The logs show:
- Cluster is initialized (n1 is node 1)
- Nodes are joining (n2 and n3 are being added)
- Migration is running ("create default databases")
- Range replication is happening (underreplicated warning is normal while nodes are being added)

The pods are still showing 0/1 Ready because the readiness probe checks `/health?ready=1` and the node is still bootstrapping. Let me wait a bit more and check again.

content.text: Initialization is progressing well — node 1 is up, nodes 2 and 3 are being replicated to. The underreplicated ranges alert is normal during bootstrap. Waiting for readiness probes to pass:
